prompt: Which vulnerability is most likely present in this code?
code_snippet: return Map.of("name", user.getName(), "passwordHash", user.getPasswordHash());  // serialised into the JSON API response

options.text: Information Exposure Through an Error Message

explanation: A sensitive field ("passwordHash", user.getPasswordHash()) is serialised straight into the API response, exposing data the client should never receive — Exposure of Sensitive Information (CWE-200). The near-miss is CWE-209, but that is specifically about *error messages*; this is a normal response. Fix: return a DTO/view model with only the fields the client needs.
